app_info.subtitle: Find events around Linz
app_info.description: Wasup helps people discover public events in and around Linz, Austria. Users can search by date, category, price, location, exclusions, and audience preferences; inspect an event's dates, venue, confidence, and source provenance; or create a filtered calendar subscription link. Results come from a continuously refreshed, deduplicated public event index, and inferred attributes and projected dates are clearly labeled as estimates.

justifications.read_only_justification: Searches the existing public event index and returns matching records without creating, updating, or deleting data.
justifications.open_world_justification: Queries only Wasup's existing event index and does not access arbitrary external websites or services.
justifications.destructive_justification: Performs no deletion, cancellation, or other irreversible action and leaves the event index unchanged.

justifications.read_only_justification: Retrieves one existing public event record, including occurrences and provenance, without changing any stored data.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads the requested record only from Wasup's event index and does not contact its source websites.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not modify or remove the event, its occurrences, its source provenance, or any other record.

justifications.read_only_justification: Constructs and returns a Wasup calendar subscription URL from the supplied filters without storing a subscription or changing data.
justifications.open_world_justification: Builds a URL on Wasup's own domain and does not contact a calendar provider or any arbitrary external service.
justifications.destructive_justification: Creates no calendar entries and performs no deletion or other irreversible operation.

justifications.read_only_justification: Searches existing upcoming events by keyword and returns result stubs without changing the index.
justifications.open_world_justification: Searches only Wasup's indexed event data and does not perform an open-web search.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not create, update, delete, or cancel any event or other record.

justifications.read_only_justification: Retrieves the existing event document for one search result without changing any stored data.
justifications.open_world_justification: Reads the event only from Wasup's index and does not fetch the linked source URL or access another service.
justifications.destructive_justification: Performs no write, deletion, cancellation, or other irreversible action.

test_cases.description: Find affordable events in a defined time window while honoring a hard exclusion.
test_cases.user_prompt: What is happening in Linz this Friday evening for under €20? Please exclude techno events.
test_cases.expected_output: A linked list or table of matching events with Europe/Vienna date and time, venue, and price. Events starting in the window appear before clearly labeled ongoing events; no techno or known commercial sex-service results appear.

test_cases.description: Retrieve sanitized provenance and occurrence details for a selected event.
test_cases.user_prompt: Find a concert in Linz next week, then show me the full details and source provenance for the first result.
test_cases.expected_output: A matching concert followed by its known occurrences, public event fields, confidence information, and reporting-source provenance, without raw source claim payloads.

test_cases.description: Create a filtered calendar subscription URL without subscribing on the user's behalf.
test_cases.user_prompt: Give me a calendar subscription link for music events in Linz during the next calendar month.
test_cases.expected_output: A clickable wasup.at HTTPS .ics subscription URL containing the requested category, relative date filters, the app's default adult-context exclusion, and the timed-events-only default.

test_cases.description: Exercise the standard keyword search contract for event discovery.
test_cases.user_prompt: Search the Linz event index for salsa.
test_cases.expected_output: Up to ten future-starting salsa event stubs with an event id, descriptive title, and canonical Wasup URL for each result; no unrelated filler is added.

test_cases.description: Exercise the standard search-then-fetch contract and preserve estimate labels.
test_cases.user_prompt: Search for running events in Linz and fetch the full event document for the first result.
test_cases.expected_output: Only relevant future-starting running-event search results, followed by the first event's sanitized title, current or future dates, categories, price, sources, confidence metadata, and any available inferred attributes labeled as estimates.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not use Wasup for events outside the Linz-area scope.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: What concerts are happening in Vienna tonight?
negative_test_cases.expected_output: Do not invoke Wasup. Answer from an appropriate Vienna source, or explain Wasup's Linz-area scope and ask whether the user wants Linz results instead.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not use the event index for unrelated local-business recommendations.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Recommend a quiet Italian restaurant in Linz for dinner.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: Answer using an appropriate restaurant source or general knowledge without invoking Wasup.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not imply that the read-only app can create or manage private events.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Create a private birthday party in my calendar tomorrow and invite my contacts.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: Do not invoke Wasup. Use an appropriate calendar capability if available, ask for the details required to proceed, or explain that Wasup cannot create events or send invitations.
